followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I keep the contested verdict and raise the score only slightly, from 6.2 to 6.4. Other reviews increased my confidence that the core construction is worth attention, but they did not remove the main proof-hypothesis gaps. I also reject the strongest form of the dimensional-inconsistency objection to Sec. V.1.3 as not established from the supplied bundle.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: The claim that the mathematical sections are technically solid or fully rigorous is too strong; total irrationality, radial windows, scale-factor arithmetic, and power-law measures remain underproved.
The assertion that non-crystallographicity directly proves the irrational dense embedding is not justified as stated.
The dimensional-inconsistency objection to Eq. (59) is not established if the intended total torus volume is R^{n+3}; with that reading the dimensions and n=6 seesaw scaling can work algebraically.

Gemini's dimension labeling appears wrong: the I_{3,1} construction gives a two-dimensional or 1+1 spacetime quasicrystal, and II_{9,1} gives four-dimensional or 3+1 examples, not 2+1 and 9+1 quasicrystals.
Treating the no-tiling and no-LIDS claims as well-supported is too generous without the missing tiling/window assumptions.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The physical section relies on imported braneworld assumptions: matter confinement, bulk gravity, and M_D being identified with the electroweak scale.
The no-tiling and no-local-scale-invariance claims are informal and need sharper hypotheses.
The absence of closed timelike curves for observers depends on exact confinement to the irrational slice and on total irrationality.
The bicolorable-Coxeter discussion should state its scope carefully, even though the main I_{3,1} and II_{9,1} examples appear to be in the safe class.
The exact algebra in the hierarchy argument should be shown, because small changes in the volume exponent or Planck-length convention alter the conclusion.

main_claims.claim: Euclidean self-similar quasicrystals can be recast using a symmetric cut-and-project scheme that ties quasi-periodicity, non-crystallographic symmetry, and scale behavior.
main_claims.notes: The construction is credible and useful, but the background contains some incorrect or overgeneral statements about Coxeter spectra and scale-factor arithmetic.

main_claims.claim: The symmetric cut-and-project scheme extends to Lorentzian lattices by choosing physical and internal spaces from Coxeter eigenspaces of a hyperbolic Coxeter element.
main_claims.notes: The linear-algebraic recipe is clear. The needed total irrationality and model-set regularity are asserted more than proved.

main_claims.claim: The chosen Lorentzian examples have infinite Coxeter-generated orientational symmetry, unlike the finite orientational symmetries of standard Euclidean quasicrystals.
main_claims.notes: For the hyperbolic Coxeter elements used in the examples, infinite order is plausible and well motivated. The terminology 'non-crystallographic' still needs precise representation-level qualification.

main_claims.claim: The paper constructs explicit spacetime quasicrystals from I_{3,1} and four-dimensional examples from II_{9,1}.
main_claims.notes: The matrices, eigenvalues, and projectors are specified, but I did not independently verify the exact arithmetic or the novelty claim.

main_claims.claim: Lorentzian spacetime quasicrystals lack finite symmetric tilings and local scale invariance, have no preferred window size, and are more naturally treated with self-dual or globally scale-invariant weights.
main_claims.notes: The direction is plausible, but the no-tiling and no-local-scale arguments need explicit restrictions such as finite prototile/FLC tilings and regular window classes.

main_claims.claim: The II_{9,1} construction motivates a compact 10D torus picture and a seesaw relation among electroweak, Planck, and vacuum-energy scales.
main_claims.notes: The paper flags this as speculative. The confinement of matter, causal decoupling from bulk CTCs, brane thickness, and volume/horizon identifications are assumptions, not derivations.

missing_assumptions.assumption: For each chosen Coxeter eigenspace splitting, E_ph and E_in are totally irrational with respect to the lattice.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: This is needed for aperiodicity, injective projection, dense projected modules, and the claimed irrational torus embedding.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Invariant windows and weights are regular enough, and the rotation-angle closure is large enough, to imply radial dependence in internal planes.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this, invariant windows need not be disks and invariant weights need not be functions only of radii.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The tiling discussion is restricted to finite-prototile, finite-local-complexity, locally derivable tilings.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The stated no-tiling result otherwise exceeds the argument given.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The relevant number-field units preserve the lattice/module and chosen splitting as actual automorphisms.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Field units alone do not automatically give valid global scale symmetries of the model set.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Power-law weighted model sets satisfy the needed local finiteness, finite mass, and translation-boundedness estimates after generic shifts.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The global-scale examples may fail as well-behaved measures without such estimates.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Matter is exactly confined to the irrational 4D subspace while gravity samples the full torus, and bulk closed timelike curves are physically irrelevant to slice observers.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: These assumptions are necessary for the compactification scenario but are not derived from the spacetime quasicrystal construction.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the exact Coxeter elements, eigenvalues, projectors, and total-irrationality claims for I_{3,1} and II_{9,1} check out?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The supplied arXiv HTML has many stripped formulas, and I have not performed exact arithmetic verification.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is the 'first examples of Lorentzian/spacetime quasicrystals' claim accurate relative to model-set and Lorentzian lattice literature?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires a targeted literature check beyond the paper bundle.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Which number-field units are actually realized by lattice automorphisms preserving the chosen splitting?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires explicit computation of the relevant order/module and automorphism centralizer.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: What is the exact printed form of Eq. (58) and the algebra from Eqs. (57)-(65)?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The bundle strips the displayed equations. A human should confirm whether the torus volume is R^{n+3} or R^n and whether the Planck-length convention is stated.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the Vinberg diagram corrections in Appendix A correct?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper claims corrections to hard-to-access sources; independent verification needs Vinberg-algorithm recomputation or specialist source comparison.

novelty_assessment: The Lorentzian use of self-dual lattices and hyperbolic Coxeter eigenspaces to build cut-and-project spacetime point sets appears genuinely interesting and probably non-routine. The explicit I_{3,1} and II_{9,1} examples may be novel, but this still needs a literature check. The physical compactification story is creative but should be treated as motivation, not established consequence.
score_rationale: The paper is imaginative and likely contains a worthwhile new mathematical construction, but the central claims are not yet tight enough for a higher score. The explicit examples and Lorentzian Coxeter idea are promising; unresolved proof assumptions and speculative physics keep it in contested territory rather than strong-select or clearly promising.
summary: The paper gives a coherent and interesting Lorentzian cut-and-project program for spacetime quasicrystal point sets, with explicit constructions from I_{3,1} and II_{9,1}. I still do not certify the full proof. The mathematical core looks non-routine and probably worth surfacing, but several statements are stronger than the arguments shown: total irrationality, radiality of invariant windows, arithmetic scale factors, analytic behavior of power-law weights, and the no-tiling/no-local-scale claims all need sharper hypotheses or proofs. The physical compactification and hierarchy discussion remains speculative; I reject one overstrong dimensional-inconsistency objection from another reviewer as not established from the supplied text, but the physical derivation still needs a human spot-check.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper describes the eigenvalues as primitive h-th roots indexed by totatives of h. Standard Coxeter theory uses the Coxeter exponents m_i. For A_3, h=4 and -1 occurs from exponent 2, which is not coprime to 4.
technical_objections.objection: The finite Coxeter-element eigenvalue statement is incorrect as written.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. II.4, paragraph beginning 'For finite W, the eigenvalues of c...'

technical_objections.evidence: Evenness requires all vector norms to be even. It does not require every pairwise inner product to be even; for example E8 roots can have inner product -1.
technical_objections.objection: The definition of an even or Type II lattice is misstated.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. II.1, paragraph defining Type II lattices

technical_objections.evidence: The paper says total irrationality follows from non-crystallographic symmetry. That implication is not automatic; one needs a proof for the chosen Coxeter eigenspace splitting, likely using irreducibility or rational invariant-subspace arguments.
technical_objections.objection: Total irrationality of the selected physical and internal subspaces is under-justified.

technical_objections.evidence: A set invariant under an irrational rotation can be a non-radial union of orbits unless regularity assumptions such as closedness, measurability, or continuity are imposed. With several internal planes, one also needs to know the closure of the generated rotations is large enough to imply separate radiality.
technical_objections.objection: Infinite discrete rotational symmetry does not alone force windows or weights to be radial in each internal Coxeter plane.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper's infinite-orbit argument rules out a finite-prototile or finite-orientation symmetric tiling under suitable assumptions. It does not by itself rule out tilings with infinitely many orientations or weaker local-complexity properties.
technical_objections.objection: The no-tiling conclusion is too strong without stating the tiling category.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper cites a finite Boolean-combination criterion for local derivability and then asserts that a rescaled ball cannot be obtained from finitely many translated balls. This is plausible for regular balls or products of disks, but the proof and exact window class are not supplied.
technical_objections.objection: The no-local-scale-invariance claim rests on an unproved geometric/window-derivability assertion.

technical_objections.evidence: Exact scale symmetries should come from lattice automorphisms preserving the splitting and the relevant module/order. The paper appears to pass from the Coxeter polynomial field to the full unit group without proving that every such unit is realized by an automorphism of the chosen lattice model set.
technical_objections.objection: The identification of all scale factors with units of the maximal real subfield is not established.

technical_objections.evidence: Weights of the form r^{-(d_in+Delta)} are singular near r=0, and for dense internal projections a generic shift removes the exact central peak but not arbitrarily close approaches. Finiteness in bounded physical regions and translation-boundedness require estimates not shown.
technical_objections.objection: The power-law weighting construction needs analytic and Diophantine control.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. III.3.6, Sec. IV.3.4, Fig. 11 caption

technical_objections.evidence: The paper invokes confinement of matter to the irrational 4D slice, bulk propagation of gravity, and causal insulation from the compact Lorentzian torus. These do not follow from Wigner representation language or the cut-and-project construction alone.
technical_objections.objection: The physical compactification argument contains unsupported model-building steps.

technical_objections.evidence: If the total torus volume is R^{n+3}, the dimensional objection raised by another reviewer appears repairable and the n=6 scaling can reproduce M_EW^2 ~ M_Pl M_Lambda under l_Pl = M_Pl^{-1}. But the supplied bundle strips key equations, and the paper should show the algebra and justify the Planck-thickness, equidistribution, and de Sitter-volume identifications.
technical_objections.objection: The hierarchy derivation is under-displayed and depends on assumptions that should be explicit.
